message:
feat(runtime): add p50/p95 rolling-window percentile telemetry for lock wait, lock hold, and cursor memory

Extend RuntimeLockMemoryTelemetrySnapshot and RuntimeMetrics with p50 and
p95 percentile fields computed over a bounded rolling window of 1024 recent
samples. Previously only avg/max aggregates were available, which masked
latency tail behavior and made SLO threshold comparison imprecise.

RuntimeMetrics changes:
- Add VecDeque<u64> ring buffers (behind StdMutex) for storage lock wait,
  storage lock hold, and cursor snapshot bytes sample streams.
- Each record_* method now also appends to the rolling buffer via
  record_bounded_sample(), which evicts the oldest entry at capacity.
- New accessor methods: p50/p95_storage_lock_wait_ms,
  p50/p95_storage_lock_hold_ms, p50/p95_cursor_snapshot_bytes.
- percentile_from_samples() helper: sorts a snapshot of the ring buffer
  and computes the requested percentile index.

RuntimeLockMemoryTelemetrySnapshot changes:
- New fields: p50_storage_lock_wait_ms, p95_storage_lock_wait_ms,
  p50_storage_lock_hold_ms, p95_storage_lock_hold_ms,
  p50_cursor_snapshot_bytes, p95_cursor_snapshot_bytes.
- lock_memory_snapshot() now populates all percentile fields.

Observation loop restructuring:
- Replace tokio::select! with explicit timeout-based pattern in the
  native event coalescer and capture relay loops. This avoids poll
  fairness issues with select! under high event throughput and makes
  the flush timing deterministic.
- Import RwLock from runtime_compat instead of tokio::sync directly,
  keeping the abstraction layer consistent.

ipc.rs: Update test fixtures and assertions for new percentile fields
in the status health payload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
